name: csv-processor
description: Parse, transform, and analyze CSV files with advanced data manipulation capabilities.
capabilities: Filter, sort, and transform CSV data
Clean data by removing duplicates and handling missing values
Generate statistical summaries and data profiles
Merge multiple CSV files with joins
Split large CSV files into manageable chunks
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill. The SKILL.md file contains educational examples and documentation with no executable code. All 94 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by code examples in markdown documentation. No Python, Ruby, or executable files exist in this skill. Standard library imports and file operations shown in documentation examples do not pose security risks.
valueStatement: CSV files often contain messy data that needs cleaning and transformation. This skill provides comprehensive tools and examples for parsing, cleaning, and analyzing CSV data efficiently.
userTitle: Process CSV files with advanced data manipulation
limitations: Requires external tools (pandas, csv-parser) for execution
Large files may need chunking for memory efficiency
Complex transformations may require custom code
Encoding issues may need manual resolution

useCases.title: Clean and analyze survey data
useCases.description: Process raw survey responses, remove invalid entries, calculate statistics, and generate summary reports.

useCases.title: Transform log files into reports
useCases.description: Parse application logs in CSV format, filter by time ranges, aggregate metrics, and export cleaned data.

useCases.title: Merge customer databases
useCases.description: Combine customer data from multiple CSV sources, remove duplicates, and standardize formats for CRM import.

promptTemplates.title: Basic CSV parsing
promptTemplates.prompt: Parse this CSV file and show me the first 10 rows with column names and data types: [attach CSV file]

promptTemplates.title: Data cleaning
promptTemplates.prompt: Clean this CSV by removing duplicate rows and filling missing values with appropriate defaults: [attach CSV file]

promptTemplates.title: Statistical analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this CSV file and provide summary statistics for all numeric columns, including mean, median, and outliers: [attach CSV file]

promptTemplates.title: Complex transformation
promptTemplates.prompt: Filter this sales data for Q4 2023, group by region, and calculate total revenue and average order value: [attach CSV file]

bestPractices: Always validate CSV structure and encoding before processing
Test transformations on a small sample before running on full dataset
Keep backups of original files before making changes
antiPatterns: Processing entire large files without chunking
Assuming default delimiter is always comma
Ignoring encoding issues until they cause errors

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports CSV, TSV, and other delimiter-separated formats. It auto-detects delimiters and handles various encodings.

faq.question: Can it handle very large CSV files?
faq.answer: Yes, use chunking for files over 100MB. Process in smaller pieces to avoid memory issues.

faq.question: Does it work with Excel files?
faq.answer: Convert Excel to CSV first using Excel or online converters. The skill focuses on plain text CSV formats.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill provides code examples only. Your data stays local when you run the provided code snippets.

faq.question: What if my CSV has encoding issues?
faq.answer: Try different encodings: UTF-8, Latin-1, Windows-1252. The skill shows how to detect and fix encoding problems.

faq.question: Can I merge multiple CSV files?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides examples for vertical concatenation and SQL-style joins based on common columns.
